update: Filestore is available in  asia-southeast3  (Bangkok). For more information, see  Filestore regions and zones .

You can now create regional Filestore instances starting at 100 GiB and scale them in 1 GiB units. The small instances feature is available under Restricted GA in  us-central1  and  us-east4  regions.
For more information, see  Small capacity Filestore instances .

You can now use the  Filestore CSI driver  to create Filestore regional instances.
For more information, see  Access Filestore instances with the Filestore CSI driver .

The  promoteReplica API  is available for promoting replicas to regular instances.
For more information, see  About instance replication .

You can now use the  Filestore CSI driver  to create Filestore instances with the NFSv4.1 protocol from the Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) cluster. This feature is available for zonal, regional, and enterprise  service tiers .
Custom performance  is now  generally available  for Filestore instances.

You can now use custom constraints with Organization Policy to provide more granular control over specific fields for some Filestore resources. For more information, see  Creating custom constraints for Filestore .
The  basic HDD extended range tier  is now  generally available  to all GKE customers through the  Filestore CSI driver .
Instance replication  is now generally available ( GA ).
Filestore is now available in Mexico ( northamerica-south1  region).
Deletion protection  is now  generally available  for Filestore instances.
NFSv4.1 protocol  support, integrated with Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory, is now generally available for zonal, regional, and enterprise instances.
Access Transparency supports Filestore in GA stage. For more information, see  Access Transparency supported services .
Filestore instances no longer require reserved capacity for certain internal operations. For more information, see  Monitoring instances .
The  regional service tier  is now  generally available .
Filestore supports  IP-based access control for your volumes . You can now use the Filestore CSI driver to configure IP-based access control at volume creation.
You can now  revert an instance to a snapshot state . This feature is  generally available  for instances created in the zonal and enterprise service tiers.
You can use  CMEK organization policies  to manage key usage with your new Filestore resources. This feature is now  generally available .
NFSv4.1 protocol support , integrated with  Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory , is now available in  Preview  for Filestore  enterprise and zonal  instances.
Filestore is now available in Johannesburg, South Africa ( africa-south1  region).
Zonal tier  backups  are now  generally available .
The  zonal service tier  is now  generally available .
Filestore is now available in Berlin ( europe-west10   region ).
Filestore enterprise singleshare backups for GKE  are now  generally available .
Filestore  enterprise tier   backups  are now  generally available .
Filestore is now available in Dammam ( me-central2   region ).
The  zonal service tier  with options for a higher or lower capacity band is now available in  Preview .
Filestore multishares for GKE  now supports up to 80 shares per instance ( GA ).
Support for  revert snapshot  operations is now available for  high scale SSD instances   ( Preview ).
Cloud EKM support for Filestore  is now  generally available .

Filestore is now available in Doha ( me-central1   region ). 
 Filestore is now available in Turin ( europe-west12   region ).
Support for Filestore as an NFS datastore for Google Cloud VMware Engine (GCVE)  is now  generally available .
High Scale tier snapshots  are now  generally available .
Filestore instance support for non-RFC 1918 IP addresses  is now  generally available .
Support for Filestore as an NFS datastore for Google Cloud VMware Engine (GCVE)  is now available in  Preview .
Filestore data is compliant with at-rest and in-use  data residency requirements  pursuant with Google Cloud terms of service.
High Scale and Enterprise tier instances now support  overlapping permissions  ( GA ).
Filestore (Basic HDD and Basic SSD tiers)  are now in compliance with the  FedRAMP Moderate  standard.
Filestore Backups for High Scale and Enterprise  tier instances is available in  Preview .
Filestore Multishares for GKE  is now  generally available .

Filestore is now available in Columbus ( us-east5   region ). 
 Filestore is now available in Dallas ( us-south1   region ). 
 Filestore is now available in Tel Aviv ( me-west1   region ).
Filestore High Scale and Enterprise tier instances now support IP address allocations with the /26 prefix.
Filestore is now available in Madrid, Spain ( europe-southwest1   region ).
Filestore is now available in Paris, France ( europe-west9   region ).
Filestore is now available in Milan, Italy ( europe-west8   region ).

Preview : Filestore Multishares for GKE provides a file service option for GKE users that require multiple, smaller NFS-mounted Persistent Volumes from a single Filestore instance. 
 Each Persistent Volume can start with just 100GiB of storage, providing a more cost-efficient option for customers.   
 For more information, see  Filestore Multishares for GKE .
Filestore High Scale SSD tier  is generally available ( GA ).
Filestore is now available in Santiago, Chile ( southamerica-west1   region ) for Basic HDD and Basic SSD instances.

You can now use  customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK)  to protect data at rest in Filestore's High Scale SSD Tier instances.  
 
 High Scale SSD instances stop and restart automatically when the state of an associated key changes 
 This feature is currently in  Preview

The following Filestore features are now  generally available  (GA): 
 
 Enterprise tier 
 Customer-managed encryption key support 
 Snapshots 
 Private services access support
Filestore snapshots now support  reverting to a snapshot .
You can now get support for preview features for Filestore. For details see the  Support  page.

Filestore's Enterprise tier now supports snapshots. A snapshot is a preserved state of your file share data that can be used to restore data. For more information, see the  snapshots documentation page .

You can now use Customer-Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) to protect all data at rest in Filestore's Enterprise tier instances. CMEK in Filestore is a  preview  feature. For more information, see  Encrypt data with customer-managed encryption keys .
Filestore is available in the northamerica-northeast2 (Toronto) region. See  Regions and zones .
Filestore now has a new  Enterprise tier , which lets you create 1–10 TiB regional instances that can scale up and down with your storage needs.
Filestore now supports  private services access , which lets you  create instances on a Shared VPC network in service projects .
Filestore is now available in Delhi ( asia-south2   region ).
Filestore is available in the europe-central2 (Warsaw) region. See  Regions and zones .
You can now secure your Filestore instances using a VPC service perimeter. For details, see  Securing instances with a service perimeter .
Filestore is now available in Melbourne ( australia-southeast2   region ).
Filestore is available in the  europe-central2  (Warsaw) region. See  Regions and zones .
IP-based access control is now generally available.

Filestore now supports access over VPN. You can mount Filestore file shares on an on-premises client and clients on a remote  VPC  with this new capability. To learn how to set up a VPN connection with Cloud VPN, see  Creating an HA VPN gateway to a Peer VPN gateway .
Filestore backups  has launched to beta. Now you can  back up your Filestore instances .
Filestore  Troubleshooting  page published. Now you can troubleshoot common Filestore issues.
High Scale SSD  tier released to beta. This new service tier for Filestore provides greater performance and lets you grow and shrink capacity between 60–320 TB.
IP-based access control  released to beta. This feature lets you control access to file shares by the IP addresses of clients.
Filestore is available in the  asia-southeast2  (Jakarta) region. See  Regions and zones .

Filestore service tier name change: 
 
 Standard  tier is now called  Basic HDD . 
 Premium  tier is now called  Basic SSD . 
 You can still use the old tier names and they continue to be supported. 
 
 This change may affect you if you use: 
* The  gcloud beta filestore  command-line tool (beta).
* The Filestore beta API (v1beta1).
* The Cloud Console with the Filestore API.
* The Cloud Console with the  gcloud  tool. 
 For details, see  New service tier names .
Learn how to create  low disk space alerts  for your Filestore instances.
Filestore is available in the  us-west4  (Las Vegas) region. See  Regions and zones .
Cloud Filestore is now available in the Seoul ( asia-northeast3 )  region .

GA release of Cloud Filestore 
 
 Cloud Filestore is now generally available for use.

Beta release of Cloud Filestore 
 
 The Beta version of Cloud Filestore is now available for use.
